race.name: Marathon des Glières
race.tagline: 42 kilometers across the Plateau des Glières — skiing on sacred ground where French Resistance fighters held the mountains against occupation in 1944.

vitals.aid_stations: Ravitaillement stations with hot drinks, cheese, bread, and energy food

climate.description: March on the Plateau des Glières at 1,440m brings variable Alpine conditions. Temperatures range from -5°C to 5°C. The plateau's relatively high altitude and northern exposure preserve snow well into spring. Exposed plateau sections can be windy.
challenges: Plateau exposure creates wind chill on open sections
Variable spring conditions — powder to crust possible
Altitude of 1,440m adds modest physiological stress
Access road to the plateau can be challenging in heavy snow

course.primary: Loop course on the Plateau des Glières, a high Alpine plateau in the Bornes Massif of Haute-Savoie. The terrain combines open plateau skiing with forested sections. Rolling profile with several sustained climbs. The historic plateau provides dramatic views of surrounding Alpine peaks.
course.surface: Machine-groomed trails on the Plateau des Glières Nordic domain
course.grooming: Good French Nordic resort grooming standards
features: Plateau des Glières is a WWII Resistance memorial site
Open plateau skiing with Alpine peak panoramas
French Resistance monument on the plateau
Haute-Savoie gastronomic culture extends to aid stations
Near Annecy — one of France's most beautiful cities

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: The WWII Resistance history gives this race profound emotional weight. Stunning Alpine scenery on the plateau. Proximity to Annecy helps accessibility. Solid mid-range event with distinctive character but limited international draw.

history.summary: The Marathon des Glières, founded around 1991, takes place on one of France's most historically significant sites. The Plateau des Glières was a stronghold of the French Resistance during World War II — in March 1944, nearly 500 maquisards held the plateau against Vichy and German forces. A massive stone monument on the plateau commemorates their sacrifice. The ski marathon honors this legacy, combining sport with remembrance on the same ground where resistance fighters operated on skis.
notable_facts: Plateau des Glières was a French Resistance stronghold in WWII
In March 1944, ~500 maquisards defended the plateau against German and Vichy forces
The National Resistance Monument stands on the plateau
Skiers literally race on the same ground where resistance fighters fought
Located in Haute-Savoie near the lakeside city of Annecy
Part of the broader French marathon ski calendar
